working_on.task: Prepare the 1.12.0 minor release for the 1.12.x maturation roadmap

recent_changes: Forge 1.12.0 now freezes one canonical four-tier release contract and feeds bounded adoption signals back into release readiness and follow-up packets.
Help, next, and dashboard now read release-tail state directly, including workflow stage, release tier, latest gate, and latest adoption signal.
Roadmap, implementation plan, execution report, and target-state references now share explicit closure rules, including a separate roadmap-complete sweep beyond the 1.12.0 cut.
VERSION plus CHANGELOG.md were bumped to 1.12.0, verify_repo passed, and build_release materialized fresh dist bundles at 1.12.0.
decisions_made: Ship 1.12.0 as a minor release because the maturation roadmap adds capability-bearing release-state, operator-guidance, and dashboard behavior.
Keep the canonical 1.12.0 release vocabulary at four tiers and require a separate roadmap-complete sweep before claiming the later maturation commits are also closed.
